items.title: Vitalik's P+N=1 formula: stablecoins without debt, liquidations, or the dollar
items.idea: Vitalik proposes splitting 1 ETH into P and N tokens via an options model. P is cash-like, N is leveraged. P+N = 1 ETH always. No loans, no liquidations, oracle consulted once at maturity. Estimated price drift 1-4%/yr. Teams already building — Vitalik June 11: 'the options thing is happening already.'
items.angle: Genuine architectural shift in DeFi. Removes continuous oracle dependence — the single biggest failure point in current stablecoin designs.
items.tweetA: Vitalik's P+N=1: split 1 ETH into a stable 'P' (cash-like) and levered 'N' (a call option). They always sum to 1 ETH. No loans. No liquidations. Oracle consulted ONCE at maturity. Even a 90% crash is survivable if price recovers by expiry. This is the most interesting stablecoin design in years. $ETH
items.tweetB: Every major DeFi collapse had one thing in common: liquidation cascades triggered by continuous oracle errors. Vitalik's new options model consults the oracle exactly once, at maturity. No targets for liquidation. Teams are already building. Estimated price drift of 1-4%/yr vs systems that can go to zero. Watch this space.

items.title: Blockworks acquires Messari for $10M — down 97% from the $300M Series B valuation
items.idea: Blockworks acquired Messari for $10M, a ~97% markdown from the $300M 2022 Series B valuation. Combined entity is 'a single system of record for all onchain assets.'
items.angle: $10M price for Messari is the most honest B2B crypto SaaS valuation signal in the market. Classic distress acquisition — cheap call option on next cycle data monopoly.
items.tweetA: Messari — once valued at $300M, the 'Bloomberg of crypto' — just sold to Blockworks for $10M. A 97% markdown. The data is worth more than the business model that monetizes it. Someone just bought a very cheap option on the next crypto bull market's B2B infrastructure.
items.tweetB: Blockworks bought Messari for $10M. The thesis is right: single system of record for all onchain assets. The $300M to $10M collapse means someone else captures the upside when crypto B2B multiples recover. Classic distress acquisition at the bottom of the SaaS cycle.

items.title: Hyperliquid AQA v2: USDC reserve yield flows directly into HYPE buybacks, no new emissions
items.idea: Hyperliquid validators passed AQA v2 routing USDC reserve yield into HYPE buybacks every 30 days. No new token emissions. Buybacks scale directly with stablecoin TVL.
items.angle: HYPE/TVL correlation is now the key metric. More stablecoin usage = more buyback pressure on HYPE with no dilution. Real revenue driving real buybacks.
items.tweetA: Hyperliquid just wired up a real revenue flywheel: USDC reserve yield funds HYPE buybacks every 30 days. No new token emissions. The bigger the stablecoin TVL, the more buyback pressure on HYPE. Closest thing in crypto to returning float income to shareholders. $HYPE
items.tweetB: HYPE up 8% this week partly on AQA v2. The mechanism: reserve yield from USDC holdings buys back HYPE, no new emissions. Watch Hyperliquid USDC TVL as the leading indicator for buyback size. This is a real fundamental signal.

items.title: AI pessimism priced: market pays less PE even as AI spenders' EPS grows — 'we are in a global productivity boom'
items.idea: After London investor meetings, Podolsky finds trepidation not opportunity. Market paying less PE for AI spenders even as EPS grows. Big spenders committed to $1T AI capex in 2027. US at epicenter of global productivity boom — bullish US stocks/dollar, bearish US bonds.
items.angle: When EPS grows and PE shrinks, you have a setup. Podolsky thinks AI is real. More contrarian: bearish US bonds — issuance wave is structural regardless of inflation path.
items.tweetA: Paul Podolsky in London: every investor acknowledges AI is transformational. Every investor is still skeptical it works. Microsoft EPS up, PE down. The market is pricing AI like a bubble even as the earnings prove it is not.
items.tweetB: The bull case: US productivity boom at the epicenter, $1T in AI capex committed for 2027, EPS growing. The contrarian call: bearish US bonds. Podolsky after London meetings: long US stocks and dollar, short duration.

charts.desc: EPS growth vs PE contraction for Mag-7 spenders

items.title: Medtronic Cardiac Ablation Solutions +124% in the US — gained 8 points of market share in a single quarter
items.idea: Medtronic FY2026: $36.4B revenue up 8.4% (strongest in 10 years). Cardiac Ablation Solutions +78% global, +124% US, gained 8 points of US market share in one quarter. Hugo robotic surgery expanding. AiBLE spinal AI FDA-approved.
items.angle: 124% growth in one quarter means a new market is being created by pulse field ablation. 8 share points in one quarter is extraordinary. Cardiac EP and robotic surgery runway is still long.
items.tweetA: Medtronic's Cardiac Ablation Solutions grew 124% in the US last quarter and gained 8 points of market share. In one quarter. This is not share theft — it is a new market being created in real time by pulse field ablation. $MDT
items.tweetB: Medtronic just posted its strongest organic revenue growth in 10 years — 6.6% Q4 organic. After years of mediocrity, MDT is back. Cardiac ablation +124% US, Hugo robotic surgery expanding, AiBLE spinal AI FDA-approved.

items.title: UK Defence Secretary + Armed Forces minister both resign; Tulsi Gabbard shares classified intelligence
items.idea: UK lost Defence Secretary John Healey, Minister for Armed Forces Al Carns, and PPS Pamela Nash in one day through resignation. Frankopan also calls Gabbard's decision to share intelligence evidence backing the bio-labs narrative — a Russian disinformation line — 'god-smacking.'
items.angle: Three simultaneous MoD resignations during active conflict is significant political risk for UK assets. Combined with GDP contraction and 13% utility bill rise in July. Watch GBP.
items.tweetA: UK just lost its Defence Secretary, Armed Forces minister, AND the MoD PPS in a single day — all resignations. During an active conflict. When the people responsible for national security walk out simultaneously during a war, 'fragile outlook' understates it. Watch GBP this weekend.
items.tweetB: Tulsi Gabbard shared actual intelligence evidence to back the bio-labs narrative — a line Peter Frankopan identifies as coming straight from Russian information operations. Former Director of National Intelligence. Oxford historian calls it 'god-smacking.' This is not a footnote.

items.title: SemiAnalysis: NVIDIA Spectrum-X CPO slipping 2+ quarters — yield math is brutal (but optical content unchanged)
items.idea: SemiAnalysis claims Spectrum-X CPO slips 2+ quarters. Vik's analysis: 32 optical engines at 95% yield = 0.95^32 = 19% overall yield. Need 99.5% for 85% yield. Key: whether CPO or NPO, optical content volume is identical — only packaging changes.
items.angle: Street sold optical names on CPO delay fears. But optical volume is unchanged. Buy-the-dip signal for $COHR $LITE on any CPO-panic selloff.
items.tweetA: SemiAnalysis: NVIDIA Spectrum-X CPO has a yield problem. 32 optical engines at 95% yield = 0.95^32 = 19% overall yield. For 85% yield you need 99.5% per-engine accuracy. Real constraint. But CPO to NPO does not change total optical content volume. $COHR $LITE
items.tweetB: The CPO vs NPO debate is irrelevant to the optical equipment thesis. Whether co-packaged or near-package optics, optical content is identical — only packaging changes. Demand for optics is 'higher than ever, just in different form factors.' Buy optical names on CPO-panic weakness.

charts.desc: 🔥 CHART WORTH SAVING: Spectrum-X vs Quantum-X architecture diagrams showing why 32-engine yield compounds badly.

items.title: SPY hammers back over 21 EMA on Iran deal — INTC breaking out of May downtrend
items.idea: SPY tested 50-day MA in 4-session decline, recovered over 21 EMA in 5 sessions on Iran deal news. Bulltard pressing INTC (sold $109 puts 300x next week), breaking downtrend from early May.
items.angle: The 50-day MA is now effectively a Trump intervention trigger. The 21 EMA is the primary timeframe indicator until it breaks. INTC setup: broken bearish trend + broader market recovery.
items.tweetA: SPY tested the 50-day, Trump dropped the Iran deal, market ripped back over the 21 EMA in 5 sessions. Fourth time in six years this exact playbook ran. The 50-day is not just support — it is a Trump intervention trigger. The 21 EMA is the only level that matters.
items.tweetB: Bulltard pressing INTC hard — breaking out of the May downtrend, sold $109 puts for next week at 300x. INTC breaking a bearish trend while the broader market recovers is the kind of setup options sellers love. $INTC

items.title: SPCX lock-up: no insider selling until +30% above $135 while retail trades freely — watch $175.50
items.idea: SPCX structure: retail trades immediately, insiders locked until +30% above $135 ($175.50). Pre-IPO derivatives implied +20%. 10-point gap the market needs to clear before distribution begins. Ethereum mainnet addresses +23% YoY while L2s collapsed.
items.angle: $175.50 is the first distribution zone. TRUMP token dynamics is the correct worst-case mental model.
items.tweetA: SPCX structure: retail trades immediately. Insiders cannot sell until +30% above $135 = $175.50. Pre-IPO derivatives implied +20%. Operators need 10 more points before they can exit. Watch $175.50 as the first distribution zone. Worst case: TRUMP token dynamics. $SPCX
items.tweetB: Ethereum mainnet active addresses up 23% YoY while almost every L2 has collapsed. While crypto chases SPCX, ETH L1 is quietly recovering its usage base. The best L1 revival signal of the year is happening during peak L2 hype. $ETH

items.title: SpaceX at $1.77T/95x sales — every comparable mega-IPO has historically top-ticked the market
items.idea: SpaceX: $1.77T vs $18.7B 2025 revenue (95x trailing sales), $4.2B operating loss. IPO alone raises more than all US IPOs combined in 2024 AND 2025. Historical comps: BP Oct 1987 (Black Monday), Blackstone Jun 2007, Glencore May 2011, Aramco Dec 2019, Coinbase Apr 2021, Rivian Nov 2021.
items.angle: Pattern is consistent enough to note. Record-setting IPOs at peak euphoria tend to be smart money selling to the public. At 95x sales with a net loss, zero margin for multiple compression.
items.tweetA: SpaceX IPO comps: BP Oct 1987 (Black Monday), Blackstone Jun 2007 (equity peak), Glencore May 2011 (commodity peak), Aramco Dec 2019, Coinbase Apr 2021 (BTC peaked day of listing). 95x sales, $4.9B net loss. Not predicting a top. Just noting the pattern is consistent across every cycle.
items.tweetB: SpaceX alone raises more than all US IPOs combined in 2024 and 2025. The record is not just 'biggest IPO' — it is 'most capital removed from the secondary market in a single event.' The supply/demand math matters when net equity supply has already turned positive.

items.title: ECB hiked rates — into a war-driven growth shock, same playbook as Trichet 2011
items.idea: ECB hiked rates this week. UK GDP -0.1% in April. European manufacturing stressed by energy costs. Mirrors Trichet double-hike in 2011 which the ECB reversed within months as eurozone crisis emerged.
items.angle: If Iran deal holds and oil falls, ECB may have committed a policy error forcing rapid reversal. EUR weakness and European duration longs are the trade.
items.tweetA: ECB just hiked rates. UK GDP contracted 0.1%. European manufacturing is getting crushed by energy costs. Hiking into a war-driven stagflation shock is the same playbook Trichet ran in 2011 right before the ECB had to reverse. Watch the reversal timeline and EUR on the way down.
items.tweetB: ECB hiking while Europe contracts is making the Fed's job easier. European growth weakens, ECB reverses, capital flows to US, dollar firms, US inflation stays contained. The Iran war's biggest macro gift to the US might be Europe's policy mistake.

items.title: UMich 48.9 (from record low 44.8): 57% spontaneously say high prices eroding their finances
items.idea: June preliminary UMich: 48.9 (May 44.8), first increase in 4 months. Still second-lowest since 1970s, 19% below year ago. 57% of consumers spontaneously mentioned high prices eroding finances (up from 36% year ago). 1-yr inflation 4.6%, 5-10yr fell sharply to 3.4% from 3.9%.
items.angle: Recovery from record lows on one month of gasoline relief is extraordinarily fragile. 57% volunteering price complaints without being asked is embedded stress not cyclical noise.
items.tweetA: UMich consumer sentiment bounced to 48.9 from the record low of 44.8. Reason: cheaper gasoline. Problem: 57% of consumers SPONTANEOUSLY mentioned high prices eroding personal finances — up from 36% a year ago. You do not bounce back from embedded price stress on one month of oil relief.
items.tweetB: UMich 5-10yr inflation expectations fell from 3.9% to 3.4% on Iran deal optimism. Good. But sentiment is 19% below a year ago, employment near record lows, home-buying at 9-month lows. The 'improvement' to 48.9 is the second-lowest reading since the 1970s.

charts.desc: UMich time series and component breakdown

items.title: Fable 5 blocks frontier AI R&D use — Anthropic uses unreleased Mythos internally to automate its own research
items.idea: Anthropic built guardrails into Fable 5 preventing external frontier AI R&D use. Internally uses Fable + unreleased Mythos to automate most AI research. Employees: 'barely written a line of code since Fable.' Fable employees: 'a third era quietly started today.' Actions from sincere safety concern look identical to actions entrench own power.
items.angle: Access to frontier AI models is now explicitly a strategic asset. The company controlling AI-for-AI-research wins the recursive improvement race. Winner-take-all dynamics intensifying.
items.tweetA: Anthropic's Fable 5 has guardrails blocking competitors from using it to build better AI. Internally, they use Mythos — still unreleased, more powerful — to automate their own AI research. Their employees barely write code anymore. The moat is recursive: best model builds next best model. No one else gets that tool.
items.tweetB: Anthropic employees: 'I have barely written a line of code since Fable.' That is not a feature — it is a job category collapsing in real time inside the lab closest to AGI. And Anthropic is blocking others from the same capability. Winner-take-all dynamics intensifying.

items.title: OpenAI calls for international organization to coordinate 'slowing frontier development when needed'
items.idea: OpenAI formally called for an international org enabling 'coordinated action, including slowing frontier development when needed.' First major frontier lab to publicly advocate for pause infrastructure. Same week as SpaceX IPO and OpenAI confidential IPO filing.
items.angle: Proposing pause-capable governance while racing to IPO is the deepest contradiction in AI policy. This proposal benefits OpenAI much more than smaller challengers.
items.tweetA: OpenAI called for an international org to coordinate 'slowing frontier development when needed.' Same week they filed confidential IPO paperwork. 'Coordinate slowdowns' translates to: make it harder for challengers to catch up after we have already won.
items.tweetB: If a pause-capable international AI org gets created, the companies with the biggest capital base and model lead survive it best. OpenAI proposing this framework NOW is not altruism — it is the playbook of a company that thinks it can outrace the regulator and still win.

items.title: White House negotiating federal preemption of state AI laws in exchange for child safety support
items.idea: White House reportedly negotiating federal preemption of some state AI laws in exchange for support on social media and AI child protection measures. Would supersede California, Colorado, New York, and dozens of other state frameworks.
items.angle: State AI laws were the biggest near-term existential risk to frontier labs. Federal preemption removes it at the cost of child protection requirements they would implement anyway. Market has not priced in the regulatory tail risk reduction.
items.tweetA: The White House is trading child safety legislation for federal preemption of state AI laws. California's bills, Colorado's, New York's — potentially nullified by one deal. Fifty fragmented regulators become one. Frontier labs will find one master easier to manage than fifty.
items.tweetB: State AI laws were the biggest near-term risk to Anthropic and OpenAI. A federal preemption deal removes it at the cost of child protection requirements they were implementing anyway. Regulatory arbitrage masterclass — and the market has not priced in the regulatory tail risk reduction.

items.title: Mortgage purchase apps +7.3% with no rate change — first-time buyers hit 35%, home sales beat
items.idea: Purchase apps +7.3% w/o/w (no rate change at 6.60%), refi +15.3% w/o/w after 6 weeks of declines. May home sales 4.17M vs 4.07M estimate. First-time buyers 35% (from 30% year ago).
items.angle: Demand bouncing without cheaper money signals genuine pent-up demand. First-time buyers at 35% structurally bullish as marginal buyer cohort. Iran deal = path to lower rates = housing catalyst.
items.tweetA: Purchase mortgage apps jumped 7.3% this week with NO rate change. 6.60% rates, same as last week. Demand bouncing without cheaper money means pent-up demand is real. First-time buyers hit 35% of all purchases (vs 30% a year ago). May home sales: 4.17M vs 4.07M estimated. $ITB $LEN $DHI
items.tweetB: If the Iran deal holds and oil falls another $10, gasoline drops, inflation eases, and mortgage rates follow. A 50bps rate decline would unlock the demand clearly pent up — first-time buyers at 35%, purchase apps bouncing without rate help. Iran deal is now the housing bull's best ally.

items.title: RH: 'Market can get worse and I'd still beat guidance' — Casey's sees growth across all income cohorts
items.idea: RH CEO: revenue will inflect past the peak investment cycle, does not need housing improvement to beat guidance. Casey's General Store: growth across all three income cohorts, lower-income slightly lagging but not deteriorating, 75% of guest base (mid/upper) spending comparably.
items.angle: RH calling their own bottom from peak-cycle stress is either supreme conviction or supreme cope. Casey's convenience fuel data is the most unfiltered consumer health signal — when this rolls over, recession has arrived. Not there yet.
items.tweetA: RH CEO: 'I'm not counting on housing getting better. The market can get worse and I'd be surprised if we don't beat our numbers.' Either supreme conviction or the best setup for a miss. But the real signal: they have already absorbed the worst of the luxury cycle. $RH
items.tweetB: Casey's General Store sees growth across ALL income levels — lower-income slightly weaker, mid/upper (75% of customer base) spending normally. Convenience fuel spend is the first thing that drops in a real consumer stress event. It has not dropped. Rolling recession, not broad collapse.

items.title: SpaceX $75B IPO: largest in history — NDX inclusion in 15 days, OpenAI and Anthropic file confidentially
items.idea: SpaceX $75B at $1.77T (555.6M shares at fixed $135). 3.5-4x oversubscribed with $250B institutional demand. Net loss $4.9B on $18.7B revenue. Nasdaq fast-track: NDX-eligible in 15 trading days. S&P excludes (no profitability). OpenAI and Anthropic filed confidential IPO paperwork after SpaceX filing.
items.angle: NDX inclusion in 15 days = forced passive buying. S&P exclusion is the interesting counterpoint. Three potential trillion-dollar listings in one quarter would be unprecedented.
items.tweetA: SpaceX gets into the Nasdaq-100 in 15 trading days — Nasdaq changed the rules for top-40 mega-caps. Passive funds MUST buy. The S&P 500 will not include them: net loss of $4.9B. But NDX inclusion alone forces massive index rebalancing. This is a mechanical bid that matters this month. $SPCX
items.tweetB: SpaceX filed. OpenAI filed confidentially. Anthropic filed confidentially. All in the same week. The smartest founders in tech are reading the same signal: the window to raise at peak valuations is open now. Three potential trillion-dollar listings in one quarter would set every record.

items.source: MacroEdge + Dark Side of the Boom + Neil Sethi
items.title: US-Iran MOU: 14 provisions, Strait of Hormuz reopening — WTI broke $84, Goldman cut forecast $5
items.idea: G7 officials + Iranian state media: draft MOU nearly finalized with 14 provisions including Strait reopening, oil sanctions lifted, 60 days nuclear negotiations. Trump: 'We ended the war with Iran today.' Iranian FM: 'not yet reached a conclusion.' WTI broke $84.46-$85.95, Brent approaching $88. Goldman cut crude forecast by $5. 40mb SPR release announced.
items.angle: Market trading peace probability not certainty. Goldman cut is the tell. Tanker flow normalization takes weeks after any deal. Oil stays weak on headline, could bounce when logistics reality hits.
items.tweetA: Goldman cut crude forecast by $5 on Iran deal. WTI broke $84. But shipping is not stocks — you cannot flip the switch. War-risk insurance reprices over weeks. Tanker repositioning takes weeks. Oil stays weak on the headline, then could bounce when the logistics reality hits the market. $USO $XLE
items.tweetB: 40mb SPR release + Iran deal headlines + Goldman demand downgrade = WTI at $83. But $XLE is consolidating, not collapsing. Energy equities have already absorbed headline risk. If the deal fails, you are long energy at cheap valuation. If it holds, the war premium bleeds out gradually over weeks.

charts.desc: WTI and Brent charts with key support levels

items.title: AI Guardrails Act: no private company or SecWar should set rules for the AI kill chain — ROBOCOM approved
items.idea: Sen. Slotkin on ChinaTalk: neither Anthropic nor a general should control the AI kill chain unilaterally. AI Guardrails Act is the legislative attempt. NDAA markup approved ROBOCOM — new Robotic and Autonomous Systems Combatant Command.
items.angle: ROBOCOM is the institutional scaffolding for autonomous warfare. Fable guardrails and AI Guardrails Act are the same question in civilian vs military clothing. The gap between the two answers is the biggest policy risk in AI.
items.tweetA: ROBOCOM: Congress just approved a new military command for robotic and autonomous weapons. Sen. Slotkin: neither Anthropic nor a general should control the AI kill chain unilaterally. The AI Guardrails Act is trying to solve who decides. Nobody has a clean answer. This is the most important question in defense.
items.tweetB: The Fable guardrails controversy and the AI Guardrails Act are the same underlying question: who decides what the most powerful AI can do? In civilian life: currently Anthropic. In military life: should be Congress. The gap between those two answers is the biggest policy risk in AI right now.

items.title: Slotkin + Moreno bipartisan bill: ban Chinese connected vehicles from US roads
items.idea: Sen. Slotkin (D-MI) and Sen. Moreno (R-OH) co-sponsoring bill banning Chinese connected vehicles. Goes beyond trade to data sovereignty — every Chinese EV is a rolling sensor package. Bipartisan support increases passage likelihood.
items.angle: If bill passes, BYD's American story ends before it starts. Real question: does Europe follow? Germany split between China market access and not wanting surveillance infrastructure on EU roads.
items.tweetA: Slotkin + Moreno: bipartisan bill banning Chinese connected vehicles from US roads. This is not trade protectionism — it is data sovereignty. Every Chinese EV on American roads is a rolling sensor package with connectivity to Beijing. Michigan auto country supporting this says everything.
items.tweetB: If the US bans Chinese connected vehicles, BYD's American story ends before it starts. The real question: does Europe follow? Germany's auto industry needs China market access for sales but does not want Chinese surveillance infrastructure on EU roads. That tension defines European EV policy through 2027.

items.title: US manufacturing 'boom-let' is entirely AI — imports growing faster than domestic capital stock since 2013
items.idea: a16z data: capital spending on structures and equipment as share of GDP declining since early 1980s. Imports growing faster than domestic capital stock. US net importer of machines since 2013. Manufacturing construction ex-AI grew only ~3% in 2025. Machinery construction: $1.5B to $3.9B (still tiny). Core industrial equipment ~50% turbines/HVAC + ~50% special industry machinery (semiconductors).
items.angle: CHIPS Act builds chip fabs. What builds the tools that supply those fabs? German machine-tool makers. US importing the machines that would allow it to stop importing machines. 10-year structural deficit.
items.tweetA: a16z charts: the entire US manufacturing investment surge is AI. Equipment ex-AI: flat. Structures ex-AI: declining as share of GDP. Machinery construction spending doubled to... $3.9B (from $1.5B). We are building AI chip factories while importing every other kind of factory. The 'reindustrialization' is one layer thick.
items.tweetB: The US was a net exporter of machinery until 2013. We now import the machines that would allow us to manufacture more machines. CHIPS Act builds fabs. What builds the tools that supply the fabs? German machine-tool makers, mostly. We have not solved the second-order manufacturing problem. Tariffs do not fix this.

charts.desc: 🔥 CHART WORTH SAVING: Capital stock vs imports divergence, machinery construction numbers, net exporter/importer inflection 2013. Visual case for why reindustrialization is incomplete.

items.title: Traditional spirits down 10% YoY — all category growth from non-alcoholic (+70%) and prepared cocktails (+26%)
items.idea: Nielsen data: spirit volumes up ~4% YoY but exclusively from non-alcoholic spirits (+70%) and prepared cocktails (+26%). Strip those out: traditional spirits DOWN ~10% YoY. Two structural forces: GLP-1 users drink materially less, Gen Z barely drinks. OKC Thunder left unopened beer after winning championship.
items.angle: Drinks category bifurcating: premium prepared cocktails + zero-proof. The loser is traditional pour-from-bottle spirits — exactly what Brown-Forman and Diageo sell.
items.tweetA: a16z data: strip out prepared cocktails and non-alcoholic spirits and traditional spirit volumes are DOWN 10% YoY. Non-alcoholic spirits: +70%. Prepared cocktails: +26%. Two trends: GLP-1 users drink less, Gen Z barely drinks. Brown-Forman and Diageo are fighting a structural demand shift. $BF.B $DEO
items.tweetB: The OKC Thunder left unopened champagne after winning the championship. The data confirms it: non-alcoholic spirits +70%, traditional spirits -10%. The drink occasion is not disappearing — the alcohol part is. Whoever owns premium non-alcoholic at scale owns the next decade.

charts.desc: Spirit volume charts by category — non-alcoholic vs traditional divergence is visually striking

items.title: Net equity supply turned positive for first time since 2021: swung from -$216B to +$124B annualized
items.idea: Fed Flow of Funds data: net equity supply swinging from -$216B annualized (Q4 2025) to +$124B annualized (Q1 2026) — first positive reading since Q1-Q2 2021. SpaceX $75B IPO is the symbol. Berkshire historically increases cash 1-2 years before companies issue large amounts of stock. Buybacks may weaken as AI capex consumes capital.
items.angle: Equity scarcity was the most underappreciated structural bull factor of 2023-2025. If scarcity is ending, market needs genuine earnings growth — not flow mechanics. Berkshire's cash pile is suddenly the most relevant leading indicator.
items.tweetA: Net equity supply just turned positive for the first time since 2021. Q4 2025: -$216B annualized. Q1 2026: +$124B. The equity scarcity that powered passive flows for three years is over. SpaceX is the headline but this is a structural shift. Berkshire has been building cash for 18 months. Not a coincidence.
items.tweetB: Buybacks were the quiet engine of the 2023-2025 bull market. Now AI capex is consuming the same capital. If buybacks slow AND new issuance rises, EPS growth via financial engineering becomes much harder. The market needs real earnings growth from here — not just share count math. The tape is getting heavier.

charts.desc: 🔥 CHART WORTH SAVING: Net equity supply flipping from negative to positive — clearest structural shift chart of the quarter.

items.title: Bitcoin ETF largest sustained selling streak since launch — Strategy buying 28,000 BTC/month
items.idea: Bitcoin ETFs in longest sustained selling streak since spot ETF launch in Jan 2024 — largest cumulative outflow streak ever. Strategy holds 845,000+ BTC, buying ~28,000 BTC/month in 2026. Monthly Strategy purchases are roughly 2x monthly Bitcoin mined (~13,500 BTC/month).
items.angle: If ETF flows reverse on Iran deal risk-on, very limited supply to absorb demand — Strategy has been soaking up available supply. Asymmetry favors sharp move up if ETF selling stops.
items.tweetA: Bitcoin ETF outflows are in their longest sustained selling streak since spot ETF launch. But Strategy is buying 28,000 BTC per month — roughly 2x monthly Bitcoin mined. Tug of war between retail ETF exit and a single corporate buyer providing the floor. Who flinches first determines the range.
items.tweetB: Strategy holds 845,000+ BTC. At current pace: 1 million BTC by year-end = nearly 5% of all BTC ever mined. If ETF flows reverse on Iran deal risk-on, there is no obvious supply to absorb demand — Strategy has been soaking it up. The asymmetry is real. But CPI at 4.2% is the ceiling for now. $MSTR

charts.desc: 🔥 CHART WORTH SAVING: Cumulative ETF flow streaks chart showing current streak dwarfs all prior selling periods.

items.title: CPI hits 4.2% — highest since 2023, rate hike debate re-enters Fed discussion
items.idea: Headline CPI rose to 4.2%, highest since 2023. Core and core services also moving higher — not energy-only. Some investors discussing whether Fed's next move could be a HIKE. Cash is becoming more attractive, financing tightening.
items.angle: If CPI is at 4.2% with Strait closed, what happens if wages stay sticky? Market's 'next move is a cut' consensus is breaking. Iran deal is now the Fed's most important economic development.
items.tweetA: CPI hit 4.2% — highest since 2023. Core AND core services both moving higher — not energy-only. Some investors now discussing whether the Fed's next move is a HIKE. Two years of 'cuts are coming' narrative is collapsing in real time. The rates market is not ready for this repricing.
items.tweetB: Best case for the Fed: Iran deal works, oil drops, CPI falls 0.5-1% on energy relief. That is the only credible path to cuts in 2026. Core services is sticky — wages are sticky. The Iran deal is now the Fed's biggest potential ally. And biggest potential problem if it falls through.

charts.desc: CPI and core inflation trend charts

items.source: Neil Sethi + Dark Side of the Boom
items.title: UK GDP -0.1% in April — sport and recreation collapsed 9.1% from Middle East event cancellations
items.idea: UK GDP -0.1% April (first decline since Aug 2025). Services -0.2%, sport/recreation -9.1% m/o/m from Middle East event cancellations. Admin/support -2.2% from weak labor demand. BOE reluctant to hike. Household utility bills rise 13% in July.
items.angle: UK sports/recreation at -9.1% is the clearest direct economic transmission from Iran war into G7 economy through unexpected channel. BOE in policy trap: hike would crush contracting growth, cut would validate inflation.
items.tweetA: UK GDP -0.1% in April. Sports and recreation collapsed 9.1% in a single month from Middle East event cancellations. The Iran war is transmitting directly into UK economic activity through the sports-events supply chain. And household utility bills rise 13% in July. The worst for UK growth is still ahead.
items.tweetB: The BOE is in the tightest bind in the G7: inflation above target from energy shocks, growth contracting, utility bills rising 13% in July, recruiters reporting weak labor demand. Rate hikes would crush an already-contracting economy. Rate cuts validate inflation. No good move — welcome to stagflation corner.

charts.desc: UK GDP monthly breakdown by sector

items.title: $25 trillion in US Treasury auctions in 2026 — $500B per week — 'after the everything bubble, the everything bust'
items.idea: US gross Treasury debt for auction in 2026 approaches $25T (~$500B/week). Debt/liquidity paradox: debt needs liquidity to refinance, liquidity depends on high-quality debt as collateral. Debt/liquidity ratios above median historically preceded crises. Treasury manages via buybacks + bill front-loading; Fed via repo liquidity.
items.angle: $500B/week is a structural demand test running every week, largely ignored. Who buys the marginal Treasury — foreign CBs? domestic banks? Fed? If any steps back simultaneously, the market discovers very quickly.
items.tweetA: $500 billion in US Treasury auctions per week. Every week. $25 trillion total in 2026 alone. The question is not whether rates stay high — it is whether the financial system has balance sheet capacity to absorb this pace. Capital Wars: 'after the everything bubble may come the everything bust.'
items.tweetB: The debt/liquidity paradox: debt needs liquidity to refinance, but liquidity depends on stable high-quality debt as collateral. When this circular logic breaks — 2008, 2020, UK gilts 2022 — it breaks fast. At $500B per week, the US Treasury auction is the biggest recurring stress test in global finance.

charts.desc: 🔥 CHART WORTH SAVING: Debt/liquidity ratio across advanced economies with crises and bubble periods marked.

items.title: Cardano Q1 2026: price -27.4%, TVL -24%, addresses -28% — while institutional stack builds toward Aug 9 ETP eligibility
items.idea: Cardano Q1: ADA -27.4% to $0.24, DeFi TVL -23.5% to $133.1M, daily addresses -28.1% to 13,400. But: stablecoin cap +27.1% to $48.6M, USDCx captured 36% of stablecoin market by quarter-end, LayerZero connecting to 160+ chains, Midnight mainnet launched, Grant Thornton Switzerland completed first global onchain financial audit. CME ADA futures launched Feb. ADA spot ETP eligibility: August 9, 2026.
items.angle: Sharpest disconnect between price and institutional infrastructure in crypto. ADA ETP eligibility is hard catalyst Aug 9. The question: does institutional framework convert to adoption, or is this a ghost chain with good compliance rails?
items.tweetA: Cardano Q1: price -27%, TVL -24%, active addresses -28%. Also: USDCx launched capturing 36% of stablecoin market in 5 weeks, first-ever onchain financial audit by Grant Thornton Switzerland, ADA spot ETP eligible August 9. The institutional build is happening while price collapses. $ADA
items.tweetB: ADA spot ETP eligibility: August 9, 2026. CME already trades regulated ADA futures. Circle launched USDCx. Grant Thornton audited onchain. At $0.24, down 27% QoQ, you are buying the institutional infrastructure before the demand shows up. Hard catalyst in 58 days. $ADA
